articles.description: Arcee is a tiny 26-person U.S. startup that built a high-performing, massive, open source LLM. And it's gaining popularity with OpenClaw users.

articles.title: VC Eclipse has a new $1.3B fund to back — and build — ‘physical AI’ startups
articles.description: Eclipse will put some of that money towards incubating, or building startups.

articles.title: Iranian hackers are targeting American critical infrastructure, US agencies warn
articles.description: A joint FBI, NSA, and CISA advisory warns that Iranian hackers have "escalated" their tactics in response to the ongoing U.S.-Israel war with Iran.

articles.title: Firmus, the ‘Southgate’ AI data center builder backed by Nvidia, hits $5.5B valuation
articles.description: Nvidia-backed Asia AI data center provider Firmus has now raised $1.35 billion in six months.

articles.title: Intel signs on to Elon Musk’s Terafab chips project
articles.description: Intel will join SpaceX and Tesla in an effort to build a new U.S. semiconductor factory in Texas, although the scope of its contributions are unclear.

articles.title: Anthropic debuts preview of powerful new AI model Mythos in new cybersecurity initiative
articles.description: The new model will be used by a small number of high-profile companies to engage in defensive cybersecurity work.

articles.title: Apple’s foldable iPhone is on track to launch in September, report says
articles.description: The news follows a report from Nikkei Asia on Tuesday that raised concerns the company’s foldable iPhone could be delayed due to challenges during the phone’s engineering test phase.

articles.title: Russian government hackers broke into thousands of home routers to steal passwords
articles.description: Fancy Bear, also known as APT28, has taken over thousands of residential home routers to steal passwords and authentication tokens in a wide-ranging espionage operation.

articles.title: Chrome finally adds a better way to deal with too many open tabs
articles.description: Google Chrome's latest update brings vertical tabs and a cleaner Reading Mode to help manage cluttered browsing.

articles.title: Hate jump scares? This app warns you before they happen
articles.description: The app uses iPhone Live Activities to alert you when scary scenes are about to happen, while also tracking what to watch and where to stream it.

articles.title: Uber is the latest to be won over by Amazon’s AI chips
articles.description: Uber is expanding its AWS contract to run more of its ride-sharing features on Amazon's chips. This is a thumb-of-the nose at Oracle and Google.

articles.title: Anthropic ups compute deal with Google and Broadcom amid skyrocketing demand
articles.description: Anthropic bulked up its compute deal with Google and Broadcom as the company has seen its run-rate revenue surge to $30 billion.

articles.title: Google Maps can now write captions for your photos using AI
articles.description: Google is rolling out new features to make it easier for users to contribute local knowledge to Maps. Most notably, Gemini can now create captions when users are looking to share a photo or video about a place.

articles.title: A teenage Minecraft YouTuber raised $1,234,567 for a meme prediction market called Giggles. It broke me.
articles.description: Justin Jin’s company Giggles – which he describes as “putting a trading app and TikTok together” – started as a joke.

articles.title: 4 days left to save close to $500 on TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 passes
articles.description: Four days left to save up to $482 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 ticket. These low rates will disappear on April 10 at 11:59 p.m. PT. Register now.

articles.title: Trump administration plans to cut cybersecurity agency’s budget by $700 million
articles.description: The budget proposal would force CISA to operate with a significantly lower budget than previous years, citing the government's claims that the election misinformation programs were used to "target the President."

articles.title: Why a former AirPods engineer is now building heat pumps
articles.description: Merino Energy has simplified the heat pump dramatically in a quest to make them cheaper and easier to install.

articles.title: The AI gold rush is pulling private wealth into riskier, earlier bets
articles.description: On a recent episode of Equity, we  talked to Arena Private Wealth to explore a growing trend: family offices bypassing VCs to gain direct exposure to AI startups, turning them from passive investors into active participants.

digest:
## 🔬 Major Technical Developments

### Project Glasswing: Securing critical software for the AI era
URL: https://www.anthropic.com/glasswing
Anthropic launches formal verification initiative for AI-critical infrastructure
Anthropic is positioning itself as the "safety lab" with teeth—Glasswing appears to be a move toward mathematically provable security guarantees for AI systems. This matters because formal verification has historically been too expensive for anything but aerospace and nuclear; if Anthropic can make it practical for AI infrastructure, it changes the liability calculus for enterprise adoption.

### System Card: Claude Mythos Preview
URL: https://www-cdn.anthropic.com/53566bf5440a10affd749724787c8913a2ae0841.pdf
New frontier model positioned for defensive cybersecurity applications
The Mythos preview paired with the Glasswing announcement suggests Anthropic is building a vertically integrated security stack. Per TechCrunch, deployment is limited to "high-profile companies"—likely defense and critical infrastructure. Watch for government contract announcements.

### When the compiler lies: breaking memory safety in safe Go
URL: https://ciolek.dev/posts/when-the-compiler-lies
Memory safety vulnerabilities discovered in Go's "safe" subset
A serious finding for the Go ecosystem. If the compiler's safety guarantees can be bypassed, this affects every Go-based infrastructure tool—Kubernetes, Docker, Terraform. Expect patches and a broader conversation about whether "memory-safe languages" are sufficient for high-assurance systems.

### Cloudflare targets 2029 for full post-quantum security
URL: https://blog.cloudflare.com/post-quantum-roadmap/
Three-year runway to quantum-resistant encryption across Cloudflare's network
2029 is aggressive but necessary—harvest-now-decrypt-later attacks mean sensitive data transmitted today is already at risk. Cloudflare's roadmap becomes a forcing function for the entire CDN/edge computing industry. Enterprises without PQ migration plans are behind.

## 💼 Business & Market Signals

### Anthropic ups compute deal with Google and Broadcom amid skyrocketing demand
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/07/anthropic-compute-deal-google-broadcom-tpus/
Run-rate revenue hits $30 billion as compute partnership expands
The $30B run-rate number is staggering—that's roughly 6x OpenAI's reported 2024 figures. Google's TPU bet is paying off as an alternative to Nvidia dependency. Broadcom's involvement signals custom silicon is now table stakes for frontier labs.

### Firmus, the 'Southgate' AI data center builder backed by Nvidia, hits $5.5B valuation
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/07/firmus-the-southgate-ai-datacenter-builder-backed-by-nvidia-hits-5-5b-valuation/
$1.35B raised in six months for Asia-focused AI infrastructure
Nvidia is building its own inference ecosystem through strategic investments. Firmus's Asia focus hedges against US-China decoupling risk. The "Southgate" framing suggests this is Nvidia's Singapore play—watch for preferential chip allocation.

### Intel signs on to Elon Musk's Terafab chips project
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/07/intel-signs-on-to-elon-musks-terafab-chips-project/
Intel joins SpaceX and Tesla in Texas semiconductor manufacturing initiative
Intel's participation is a lifeline—they need manufacturing volume to justify fab investments. The unclear "scope of contributions" language suggests Intel may be providing foundry services rather than equity. This is effectively a CHIPS Act arbitrage play with Musk's political capital.

### VC Eclipse has a new $1.3B fund to back — and build — 'physical AI' startups
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/07/vc-eclipse-has-a-new-1-3b-to-back-and-build-physical-ai-startups/
Eclipse pivots to incubation model for robotics and embodied AI
The "build" component is key—Eclipse is acknowledging that physical AI startups need more hands-on support than software. $1.3B for incubation means they're betting on 5-10 year horizons. This is patient capital for a space that's burned impatient capital repeatedly.

## 📋 Case Studies & Deep Dives

### S3 Files and the changing face of S3
URL: https://www.allthingsdistributed.com/2026/04/s3-files-and-the-changing-face-of-s3.html
AWS repositions S3 as a filesystem, not just object storage
Werner Vogels doesn't write casually—this signals a strategic shift. S3 moving toward file semantics threatens the entire cloud storage middleware layer (NetApp, Pure Storage cloud offerings). Enterprises running NFS/SMB migrations to cloud just got a simpler path.

### Uber is the latest to be won over by Amazon's AI chips
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/07/uber-is-the-latest-to-be-won-over-by-amazons-ai-chips/
Uber expands AWS contract, shifts inference workloads to Trainium/Inferentia
The "thumb-of-the-nose at Oracle and Google" framing undersells this—Uber is a bellwether for ride-sharing and logistics AI workloads. If Amazon's custom silicon delivers acceptable latency for real-time pricing and routing, the Nvidia moat narrows considerably for inference-heavy applications.

### Why a former AirPods engineer is now building heat pumps
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/07/why-a-former-airpods-engineer-is-now-building-heat-pumps/
Merino Energy applies consumer electronics manufacturing discipline to HVAC
This is the "Tesla for heat pumps" thesis executed by someone who actually shipped hardware at scale. Heat pump installation complexity is the adoption bottleneck—if Merino can solve that, they're positioned for the IRA subsidy wave. Watch for HVAC incumbent acquisition interest.

## ⚡ Signal vs. Noise

The through-line this week is infrastructure hardening—Anthropic betting on formal verification, Cloudflare racing toward post-quantum, AWS consolidating the storage layer. The capital is flowing to picks-and-shovels plays (Firmus, Eclipse physical AI) rather than pure software bets, suggesting smart money sees the AI application layer as commoditizing faster than expected. Meanwhile, the CISA budget cuts create an asymmetric opportunity: private cybersecurity spend will accelerate as federal protection retreats, and the Anthropic/Google/Cloudflare security stack is positioning to capture that demand.
